title: Performance Review Process and Self-Assessment Tips
text:
Q: What is the performance review process, timeline, and how can I effectively complete my self-assessment?
A: The performance review process occurs annually, typically starting in January and concluding by the end of March. Employees will receive a notification in early January to begin the self-assessment phase, which lasts until the end of January. During this time, you should reflect on your accomplishments, challenges, and areas for growth over the past year. It is essential to provide specific examples and quantify your achievements where possible. After self-assessments are submitted, managers will review them and schedule one-on-one meetings in February to discuss performance, set goals, and provide feedback. The final performance reviews will be completed by the end of March, and results will be communicated to employees shortly thereafter. For assistance or questions regarding the review process, please contact your HR representative or refer to the HR portal for additional resources.
